title: NASA Unveils Initiatives to Achieve America’s National Space Policy
summary: As part of its “Ignition” event on Tuesday, NASA announced a series of transformative agencywide initiatives designed to achieve President Donald J. Trump’s National Space Policy and advance American leadership in space. These actions reflect the urgency of the moment, but also the tremendous opportunity ahead for world-changing science and discovery. “NASA is committed to […]

title: SES targets 28 satellites with K2 Space for next-gen MEO network
summary:
SES announced plans March 24 to order an initial 28 satellites from manufacturing startup K2 Space for meoSphere, a next-generation MEO network slated to be in operation by 2030.
The post SES targets 28 satellites with K2 Space for next-gen MEO network appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Orbital data centers, part 1: There’s no way this is economically viable, right?
summary: "This is not physically impossible; it’s only a question of whether this is a rational thing."

title: Fusion engine for missile warning lacks a government dataset
summary:
WASHINGTON – National missile warning and tracking could be improved if all government agencies shared the raw data they gather. “There are agencies that have sensors that can provide data that would be very supplemental to the missile-defense mission, but they designed it for a completely different purpose,” Devin Elder, Northrop Grumman Strategic Space Systems […]
The post Fusion engine for missile warning lacks a government dataset appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: XRISM solves famous star’s 50-year mystery
summary: An invisible companion consuming material from the naked-eye star gamma-Cas has been revealed as the culprit for curious X-rays coming from the stellar system. This closes the case on a mystery that has puzzled astronomers for more than fifty years.

title: US-Based Katalyst Selects Ariane 6 to Launch Satellite Servicing Spacecraft
summary:
Arizona-headquartered space operations company Katalyst Space Technologies has signed a contract with Arianespace to launch its NEXUS-1 satellite servicing spacecraft aboard an Ariane 6 in 2027. The NEXUS-1 spacecraft is designed to operate in geostationary orbit (GEO), docking with satellites to perform a range of in-orbit servicing duties, including installing new hardware, repositioning satellites, and […]
The post US-Based Katalyst Selects Ariane 6 to Launch Satellite Servicing Spacecraft appeared first on European Spaceflight.

title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle Crosses Australia
summary: The powerful storm lashed the northern edge of the continent with damaging winds and drenching rain as it made landfall multiple times.
